jobs.title: Software Engineering Intern
jobs.description:
Organization: Irenix Empowerment Foundation
Department: Innovation & Technology
Reports To: Director of Technology
Location: 100% Remote
Term: 3–6 months (summer, fall, or spring)
Time Commitment: 10–20 hrs/week
Compensation: Unpaid internship — no exceptions at this time. (Academic credit may be arranged with your institution.)
About Irenix Empowerment Foundation
Irenix Empowerment Foundation is a Wyoming-based nonprofit organized for charitable and educational purposes. Our initiatives include developing technology that expands access to care and community resources in underserved populations.
Why Join
Mentored by a NASA engineer. Learn directly from an active NASA software developer working on satellite ops and ISS systems.
Work with an AI PhD. Apply machine learning to nonprofit use cases (analytics, automation, decision support).
Mission-driven focus. Build tools that advance mental health and community well-being, with privacy and ethics at the core.
What You’ll Build
Full-stack features. Develop front-end and back-end solutions in JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, or Java.
Data & storage. Design relational/NoSQL schemas; write secure, performant queries.
APIs & microservices. Create clean, well-documented REST endpoints that connect our ecosystem.
Quality & automation. Write tests, support CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions/Jenkins), and streamline cloud deployments.
Agile teamwork. Join sprint planning, stand-ups, and retros; contribute to documentation and collaboration tools.
Mentorship deep-dives. Attend weekly tech talks and code clinics with the NASA mentor and AI lead.
You Are
Enrolled in a BS/MS program in CS, Software Engineering, or related field.
Comfortable with a modern FE framework (React/Angular/Vue) and a BE stack (Express/Django/Spring Boot).
Proficient with Git; interested in CI/CD, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP).
A clear, proactive communicator who values clean, tested code and peer review.
Nice-to-Have
Interest in regulated/ethical domains (HIPAA, privacy-by-design).
Familiarity with Jest, PyTest, or JUnit.
Exposure to DevOps/IaC/observability (Terraform, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, etc.).
What You’ll Gain
Resume-level experience: NASA mentorship + nonprofit tech portfolio.
AI in practice: Hands-on ML/automation experience with real beneficiaries.
Networking: Collaborate with seasoned engineers and a motivated peer group.
Purpose-driven work: Build software that directly supports access to care and empowerment.
Internship Standards (Nonprofit/FLSA-Aligned)
This internship is for educational purposes only. It is unpaid with no exceptions, does not displace staff, and carries no promise of employment. We provide defined learning goals, a supervisor, flexible scheduling, and evaluations as requested.
How to Apply
Email your resume and a brief cover letter to info@irenix.org with the subject line “Software Engineering Intern — Irenix Empowerment Foundation.” Rolling review; ideal start October 2025.

companies.name: Irenix Empowerment Foundation
companies.description: Irenix Empowerment Foundation is a nonprofit research and development organization dedicated to building innovative solutions that improve health, education, and opportunity in underserved communities. We are currently developing technology-driven initiatives, designs that we hope will expand access to mental health care and other wraparound services.
